V-M-079.scope: Premium Resend email template verification for verification/reset flows, Matrix-compatible transactional layout, public body-logo rendering, text fallback, token redaction, provider boundary safety, and protected-surface isolation.

module-checks.check-1: Audit current verification and password-reset HTML/text templates and identify shared layout primitives without changing Resend payload semantics.
module-checks.check-2: cd backend && SECRET_KEY=test-secret-key-with-enough-length DATA_ENCRYPTION_KEY=dpFmfSVqfdAK3yx5MQV6Tcv35RZuw5MzaH0yhxJ01q0= PYTHONPATH=. ../.venv/bin/python -m pytest -o addopts='' tests/test_email_delivery.py -v
module-checks.check-5: node scripts/phase63-visible-logo-integration-smoke.mjs remains a post-commit regression because its Phase-63 protected guard intentionally rejects uncommitted backend/email diffs.
module-checks.check-6: python3 -m xml.etree.ElementTree docs/plans/Phase-64.xml docs/modules/M-079.xml docs/verification/V-M-079.xml docs/verification-index.xml docs/graph-index.xml docs/plan-index.xml
module-checks.check-7: mygrace lint; git diff --check; protected diff guard for deploy/install, nginx, docker-compose, VPN runtime, MTProto runtime, Resend provider env, DNS/BIMI, and secret surfaces
module-checks.check-8: node scripts/phase66-minimal-email-template-smoke.mjs proves removed-copy, larger-logo, no-green-top-strip, transparent outer-background, CTA/fallback preservation, token redaction, and protected-surface guards.
module-checks.check-9: Phase-66 backend test update asserts both verification and reset template outputs keep CTA/fallback URLs while removing operator-specified verbose copy from HTML and text outputs.
module-checks.check-10: Phase-66 protected guard rejects deploy/install, nginx, docker-compose, VPN, MTProto, billing, auth-token, EMAIL_FROM, Resend-provider, DNS/BIMI, certificate, and secret-surface changes.
module-checks.check-11: Phase-66 docs evidence redacts verify/reset tokenized URLs and avoids real recipient addresses, provider keys, and full action links.

verification-matrix.target-1: Verification and password-reset HTML share a premium KrotPN Matrix email shell while preserving one-time token links, pending-registration activation, and password-reset semantics.
verification-matrix.target-2: Text fallback stays plain, readable, independent of remote images, free of logo URL noise, and safe for image-blocked clients.
verification-matrix.target-3: HTML uses email-client-safe structure and inline/limited CSS only; no external CSS, JavaScript, canvas, web fonts, or runtime Matrix effects are required for comprehension.
verification-matrix.target-4: Body logo uses an approved public asset path such as FRONTEND_URL plus /brand/email-logo.png and does not claim control over Gmail/Apple/Resend sender avatars, BIMI provider display, or DNS state.
verification-matrix.target-5: Tokens, full tokenized URLs, provider API keys, passwords, and secret-bearing env values are redacted from logs, provider error details, smoke output, and documentation evidence.
verification-matrix.target-6: No deploy/install, nginx, docker-compose, VPN, MTProto, billing, auth policy, EMAIL_FROM, Resend API key, DNS, BIMI, or certificate behavior changes are introduced.
verification-matrix.target-7: Verification/reset templates remove KrotPN Matrix Access, Russian eyebrow/intro/support/footer noise, #5cf2c8 top-strip, and #04090d outer-wrapper background while preserving the dark card, larger body logo, CTA, and fallback link.
verification-matrix.target-8: Phase-66 smoke distinguishes outer-wrapper background removal from dark-card preservation so transparent mailbox backgrounds do not hide the transactional card.

phase-66-negative-copy-assertions.verification-email-forbidden-3: Один шаг подтверждает, что эта почта принадлежит вам, и подготавливает аккаунт KrotPN.
phase-66-negative-copy-assertions.verification-email-forbidden-4: Удаленные изображения необязательны: ссылки ниже достаточно для подтверждения.
phase-66-negative-copy-assertions.verification-email-forbidden-5: Не пересылайте ее в чаты или сообщения поддержки.
phase-66-negative-copy-assertions.verification-email-forbidden-6: KrotPN account security message. No password or payment data is requested in this email.
phase-66-negative-copy-assertions.reset-email-forbidden-3: Используйте одноразовый маршрут, чтобы задать новый пароль для аккаунта KrotPN.
phase-66-negative-copy-assertions.reset-email-forbidden-4: Ссылка восстановления работает даже если почтовый клиент блокирует изображения.
phase-66-negative-copy-assertions.reset-email-forbidden-5: Не пересылайте ее в чаты или сообщения поддержки.
phase-66-negative-copy-assertions.reset-email-forbidden-6: KrotPN account security message. No password or payment data is requested in this email.

phase-66-style-assertions.style-1: Larger body logo is required for verification and reset templates; execution may choose the exact pixel value but must prove it increased from the Phase-64 baseline and remains email-client safe.
phase-66-style-assertions.style-2: The card edge must not contain #5cf2c8 or an equivalent green strip marker.
phase-66-style-assertions.style-3: The outer body/wrapper must not set #04090d as its background; the dark card surface remains present.
phase-66-style-assertions.style-4: No external CSS, JavaScript, canvas, web font, animated Matrix runtime, or remote dependency is allowed for critical email comprehension.

phase-66-preservation-assertions.preserve-1: Verification email keeps a localized subject, CTA button, plain text fallback, and fallback action URL.
phase-66-preservation-assertions.preserve-2: Password reset email keeps a localized subject, CTA button, plain text fallback, and fallback action URL.
phase-66-preservation-assertions.preserve-3: Action links keep one-time token semantics but token values are redacted from logs, smoke output, docs evidence, and provider error details.
phase-66-preservation-assertions.preserve-4: Public body-logo path remains inside the approved M-069/M-080 brand asset boundary and does not claim BIMI/sender-avatar behavior.

success-scenarios.scenario-1: Verification and reset emails render with compact premium KrotPN branding, visible body logo, clear CTA button, and short security copy while keeping the transactional purpose obvious.
success-scenarios.scenario-2: Mailbox clients that block remote images still expose all important instructions in HTML and text fallback.
success-scenarios.scenario-3: Backend tests prove template payload shape, recipient/subject semantics, one-time token delivery, reset flow safety, and provider-disabled behavior remain compatible.
success-scenarios.scenario-4: Phase-64 smoke proves public logo URL use, email-client-safe CSS constraints, token redaction, text fallback cleanliness, and protected-surface isolation.
success-scenarios.scenario-5: Phase-66 smoke proves the operator-specified copy is absent from verification/reset HTML/text outputs and that the minimal card still has a clear action path.
success-scenarios.scenario-6: Phase-66 smoke proves the larger-logo, no-top-strip, and transparent-outer-wrapper requirements without changing Resend payload shape or deploy/provider configuration.

failure-scenarios.failure-1: Email template depends on images, remote CSS, JavaScript, canvas, web fonts, or visual-only Matrix effects for critical meaning.
failure-scenarios.failure-2: Text fallback includes logo URL noise, HTML fragments, raw tokens, raw tokenized links, or confusing visual copy.
failure-scenarios.failure-3: Template changes alter Resend deployment/config semantics, EMAIL_FROM, provider API key handling, domain DNS/BIMI behavior, auth policy, or one-time token semantics.
failure-scenarios.failure-4: Smoke output, tests, logs, or docs print reset/verify token values, full tokenized URLs, secrets, credentials, or real recipient addresses.
failure-scenarios.failure-5: Phase-64 touches deploy/install, nginx, docker-compose, VPN, MTProto runtime, billing, tariffs, payment provider, certificate, or DNS surfaces without a new approved phase.
failure-scenarios.failure-6: Phase-66 removes the CTA button, removes the fallback link, hides the card on transparent clients, logs tokenized URLs, or changes Resend/provider/deploy/runtime behavior.

telemetry-requirements.allowed: Template name, subject class, rendered byte length, approved public asset path, logo-size marker, removed-copy marker, top-strip/background marker, redacted-token marker, text fallback marker, email-client-safe CSS marker, command pass/fail marker, and protected-surface diff summary.
telemetry-requirements.forbidden: Passwords, reset tokens, verification tokens, full tokenized URLs, access tokens, refresh tokens, API keys, Resend/YooKassa secrets, WireGuard private keys, MTProto secrets, full proxy links, real recipient email addresses, raw user IPs, certificate private keys, and server credentials.
telemetry-requirements.redaction: Any evidence that references a tokenized verification/reset URL must use a redacted placeholder and must not include query strings or raw token material.

phase-gates.gate-1: M-079 execution passed backend email tests and Phase-64 template smoke before template changes were accepted.
phase-gates.gate-2: M-040 email delivery, M-062 onboarding/password recovery, M-069 email logo assets, M-072 premium visual tokens, and M-080 visible-logo boundary remain compatible.
phase-gates.gate-3: XML parse, MyGRACE lint, git diff --check, and protected deploy/install/runtime/provider/DNS guard passed before Phase-64 moved to done-local.
phase-gates.gate-4: Clean-deploy validation may send a real Resend email to inspect delivered rendering, but local verification must not claim mailbox sender-avatar/BIMI provider display proof.
phase-gates.gate-5: Phase-66 negative-copy and style assertions were added before accepting the simplified templates.
phase-gates.gate-6: Phase-64 and Phase-51 regression smokes were rerun to prove premium email and brand asset baselines are not regressed.
phase-gates.gate-7: XML parse, MyGRACE lint, git diff --check, and protected deploy/provider/runtime guard passed before Phase-66 moved to done-local.

execution-evidence.command-1: cd backend && SECRET_KEY=test-secret-key-with-enough-length DATA_ENCRYPTION_KEY=dpFmfSVqfdAK3yx5MQV6Tcv35RZuw5MzaH0yhxJ01q0= PYTHONPATH=. ../.venv/bin/python -m pytest -o addopts='' tests/test_email_delivery.py -v
execution-evidence.command-4: python3 XML parse, mygrace lint, git diff --check, protected deploy/provider/runtime/frontend guard
execution-evidence.result: PASS: premium verification/reset email templates render a public body logo, compact Matrix-safe shell, image-blocking-safe fallback panel, clean text fallback, token-safe logs, and unchanged provider/deploy/runtime surfaces.

phase-66-execution-evidence.command-1: cd backend && SECRET_KEY=test-secret-key-with-enough-length DATA_ENCRYPTION_KEY=redacted-test-key PYTHONPATH=. ../.venv/bin/python -m pytest -o addopts='' tests/test_email_delivery.py -v
phase-66-execution-evidence.command-5: XML parse, MyGRACE lint, git diff --check, and protected deploy/provider/runtime guard
phase-66-execution-evidence.result: PASS: minimal Resend template polish removed requested verbose copy and visual strip/background noise while preserving action links, text fallback, logo boundary, token redaction, and provider/deploy isolation.

V-M-079.autonomous-execution-safety: Phase-66 remained safe for local autonomous execution because changes stayed inside backend email template/tests, Phase-64/Phase-51/Phase-66 smoke scripts, and MyGRACE docs; installer/deploy, Resend provider env, DNS/BIMI, auth-token semantics, billing, VPN/MTProto runtime, and secret-handling surfaces were not changed.
